name: Mushroom Essence
recipeInstructions:
One oz. equals 2/3 cup. Recommended mushrooms include cepes, porcini, Steinpilze, or boletes sold as "Chilean dried mushrooms." Place the mushrooms in a bowl with 4 cups very warm water. Submerge them and weigh down with a saucer; let soak overnight or at least 6 hours until soft.
1 Stir occasionally to soak mushrooms.
2 Remove mushrooms from liquid using a slotted spoon (save), keeping as much juice as possible.
3 Add remaining 2 cups of water in a saucepan, simmer covered for 20 minutes.
4 Strain mushrooms and their cooking liquid through a sieve over bowl lined with fine nylon net or two layers of cheesecloth. Press firmly to extract all liquid; discard solids.
5 Combine strained mushroom liquid with shallots, peppercorns, whole mace, ground mace, and bay leaf in saucepan. Bring mixture to boil over medium-high heat then simmer briskly uncovered until reduced to 2 cups.
6 Strain solids out; allow concentrated essence to settle before decanting cooled liquid into freezer containers. Freeze in an ice-cube tray, then transfer cubes to airtight container. Keeps up to one year frozen. Yield: 16 cubes (~2 tablespoons each).
